deck.name: Health & Wellness
deck.description: Vocabulary for physical health, mental well-being, fitness, and medical situations.

cards.definition: state of emotional, physical, and mental exhaustion caused by excessive stress
cards.context: Taking a vacation is essential to avoid burnout.

cards.definition: the quality or state of being conscious or aware of something
cards.context: Mindfulness meditation can help reduce anxiety.

cards.definition: the capacity to recover quickly from difficulties
cards.context: Building resilience helps you deal with life's challenges.

cards.definition: an instruction written by a medical practitioner for a medicine
cards.context: The doctor gave me a prescription for some antibiotics.

cards.definition: a thorough medical examination to ensure you are healthy
cards.context: It's important to have an annual check-up.

cards.definition: a physical or mental feature indicating a condition of disease
cards.context: A high fever is a common symptom of the flu.

cards.definition: the identification of the nature of an illness by examination of the symptoms
cards.context: The doctor's diagnosis was a simple chest infection.

cards.definition: the size or frequency of a dose of a medicine
cards.context: Make sure to follow the recommended dosage on the label.

cards.definition: a secondary, typically undesirable effect of a drug or medical treatment
cards.context: Drowsiness is a possible side effect of this medication.

cards.definition: persisting for a long time or constantly recurring
cards.context: He suffers from chronic back pain.

cards.definition: present or experienced to a severe or intense degree
cards.context: She felt an acute pain in her knee during the run.

cards.definition: swelling, redness, and pain in a part of the body
cards.context: Ice can help reduce inflammation after an injury.

cards.definition: spending a lot of time seated; somewhat inactive
cards.context: A sedentary lifestyle increases the risk of heart disease.

cards.definition: relating to the heart and blood vessels
cards.context: Running is great for cardiovascular health.

cards.definition: chemicals produced by the body to relieve stress and pain
cards.context: Exercise triggers the release of endorphins.

cards.definition: the chemical processes that occur within a living organism in order to maintain life
cards.context: Some people naturally have a faster metabolism than others.

cards.definition: the process of absorbing water into the body
cards.context: Staying hydrated is crucial during a workout.

cards.definition: a period of gentle exercise to prepare for more intense activity
cards.context: Never skip your warm-up before lifting weights.

cards.definition: a period of low-intensity activity following more intense exercise
cards.context: A good cool-down helps prevent muscle soreness.

cards.definition: Short for series of repetitions of an exercise
cards.context: I did three sets of ten reps for my bicep curls.

cards.definition: the ability to sustain prolonged physical or mental effort
cards.context: Swimming is excellent for building stamina.

cards.definition: products taken to add nutritional value to the diet
cards.context: I take vitamin D supplements during the winter.

cards.definition: food that has been altered from its natural state for safety or convenience
cards.context: Try to limit your intake of highly processed foods.

cards.definition: food that has been processed or refined as little as possible
cards.context: A diet rich in whole foods is generally healthier.

cards.definition: the state of being comfortable, healthy, or happy
cards.context: Mental well-being is just as important as physical health.

cards.definition: the practice of taking action to preserve or improve one's own health
cards.context: Self-care isn't selfish; it's necessary for balance.

cards.definition: treatment intended to relieve or heal a disorder
cards.context: Going to therapy can provide valuable tools for coping with stress.

cards.definition: a strategy to manage or deal with difficult situations
cards.context: Journaling is a healthy coping mechanism for many people.

cards.definition: habits and practices that are conducive to sleeping well on a regular basis
cards.context: Limiting screen time before bed is part of good sleep hygiene.

cards.definition: long life; living for a long time
cards.context: Healthy habits in your 20s contribute to longevity.

cards.definition: medical services that include check-ups, counseling and screenings to prevent health problems
cards.context: Regular screenings are a key part of preventative care.

cards.definition: dealing with the whole of something or someone, not just a part
cards.context: A holistic approach to health considers both mind and body.

cards.definition: relating to, involving, or requiring free oxygen (cardio)
cards.context: Cycling and swimming are excellent aerobic exercises.

cards.definition: relating to or involving exercise which does not use oxygen (strength training)
cards.context: Weightlifting is a form of anaerobic exercise.

cards.definition: an eating pattern that cycles between periods of fasting and eating
cards.context: He's been trying intermittent fasting to improve his energy levels.

cards.definition: nutrients that the body needs in large amounts (carbs, proteins, fats)
cards.context: It's important to balance your macronutrients for optimal performance.

cards.definition: vitamins or minerals taken to add to the diet
cards.context: I take vitamin D supplements during the winter months.

cards.definition: the position in which someone holds their body when standing or sitting
cards.context: Good posture is essential for preventing back pain.

cards.definition: the study of people's efficiency in their working environment
cards.context: An ergonomic chair can help reduce strain while working from home.

cards.definition: the natural internal process that regulates the sleep-wake cycle
cards.context: Exposing yourself to sunlight in the morning helps regulate your circadian rhythm.

cards.definition: habitual sleeplessness; inability to sleep
cards.context: Stress is a common cause of temporary insomnia.

cards.definition: the action of restoring someone to health or normal life through training and therapy
cards.context: After the surgery, he spent several weeks in physical rehab.

cards.definition: clinical measurements, specifically pulse rate, temperature, respiration rate, and blood pressure
cards.context: The nurse checked the patient's vital signs.

cards.definition: having a personality or ability that is fully developed in all aspects
cards.context: A well-rounded fitness routine includes both cardio and strength training.

cards.definition: a diary where one records things for which they are grateful
cards.context: Keeping a gratitude journal helped her focus on the positive aspects of her life.
